tools.name: code_execute
tools.description:
Execute code in a sandboxed environment with no filesystem, network, or system access. Two language lanes are supported via the optional `language` parameter (default `javascript`):

• `language:javascript` — isolated Node.js vm sandbox. Available globals: Math, JSON, Array, Object, String, Number, Boolean, Date, RegExp, Map, Set, parseInt, parseFloat, console.log. 5-second default timeout (max 10 s), 8 KB output limit.

• `language:python` — Python 3.12 sidecar with stateful per-`session_id` namespace and the canonical scientific stack (#674): numpy, pandas, scipy, sympy, mpmath, statsmodels, networkx, matplotlib, seaborn, plotly, tqdm, requests, ecdsa, PuLP, astropy, qutip, control, biopython, pubchempy, dendropy, chess, mido, MIDIUtil. Honestly deferred (NOT installed): rdkit, pyscf, pygame, polygon, torch, python-snappy. Live inventory available at the sidecar `/health` endpoint. 30-second default timeout (max 120 s).

code.description: Source code to execute. JavaScript by default; Python when `language: 'python'`. No require/import of arbitrary modules, no filesystem, no network access. The Python lane preserves namespace state across calls when the same `session_id` is supplied.

language.description: Execution lane. `javascript` (default) runs in an isolated Node vm. `python` routes to the Python 3.12 REPL sidecar (canonical scientific inventory; live state of installed vs deferred libraries available at the sidecar /health endpoint).

session_id.description: Optional. For `language:python` only — supplies a stable session id so variables, imports, and dataframes persist across calls within the same swarm turn or session. Omit to create a fresh namespace.

timeout_ms.description: Maximum execution time in milliseconds. JavaScript: default 5000, max 10000. Python: default 30000, max 120000.

useWhen: the user wants to run code or a script
the user needs data transformation, sorting, or filtering
the user wants algorithmic computation that goes beyond simple math
the task requires iterating over data, string manipulation, or JSON processing
a swarm agent needs to compute, analyze, or transform structured data
avoidWhen: simple arithmetic that math_solve handles directly
the task requires filesystem, network, or system access
the task requires a Python library that is honestly deferred (rdkit, pyscf, pygame, polygon, torch, python-snappy) — check the sidecar /health endpoint first
tools.returnsSummaryShape: object with status (ok|error), output (array of console.log lines), result (last expression value as string), executionMs, truncated (boolean)

shortDescription: Execute JavaScript or Python code in an isolated sandbox with no system access — for data analysis, algorithms, scientific computing, and computation

discoveryHints.primaryCapability: sandboxed JavaScript or Python code execution
commonUseCases: scientific computing with the canonical Python stack (numpy, pandas, scipy, sympy, statsmodels, plotly, etc.)
stateful multi-step Python work within a single swarm turn via session_id
limitations: no filesystem, network, or system access in either lane
JavaScript: 5-second default timeout (max 10 seconds), 8 KB output
Python: 30-second default timeout (max 120 seconds), single sidecar process
Python deferred libraries (NOT installed): rdkit, pyscf, pygame, polygon, torch, python-snappy — see /health for live inventory

orchestratorUseCases.scenario: Swarm agent needs to analyze structured data returned by another tool
orchestratorUseCases.explanation: Agent searches for data, then uses code_execute to sort, filter, or compute statistics on the results

orchestratorUseCases.scenario: Agent needs to transform JSON data into a different structure
orchestratorUseCases.explanation: Agent writes JavaScript to restructure, merge, or pivot data
